Details: Similar to other advanced oxidation technologies that create hydroxyl radicals, ozone combined with hydrogen peroxide has been shown to be effective for 1,4-dioxane removal (8 to greater than 99 percent removal). The removal efficiency depends on ozone dose, hydrogen peroxide dose, contact time, 1,4-dioxane concentration and competing compounds, such as bicarbonate alkalinity and organic chemicals. However, most ozone and hydrogen peroxide doses tested were higher than is typical for drinking water treatment.
